---
title: "Missclasification matrix"
author: "Emilio Otero"
date: "2026-03-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
source("stat_func.R")
source("pupulation_model.R")
source("thikanov_inv.R")
library(xtable)
library(glmnet)
library(purrr)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
```

```{r}
# My missclass matrix
missclass_matrix_A <-  truncated_misclassification(func_sigma = function(x) 1, func_mu = function(x) x)
missclass_matrix_B <-  truncated_misclassification(func_sigma = function(x) 4, func_mu = function(x) x)
missclass_matrix_C <-  truncated_misclassification(func_sigma = function(x) 1, func_mu = function(x) x+4/(x+0.5))
missclass_matrix_D <-  truncated_misclassification(func_sigma = function(x) 4, func_mu = function(x) x+4/(x+0.5))

missclass_all_t <- list(
  A = t(missclass_matrix_A),
  B = t(missclass_matrix_B),
  C = t(missclass_matrix_C),
  D = t(missclass_matrix_D)
)

missclass_all <- list(
  A = missclass_matrix_A,
  B = missclass_matrix_B,
  C = missclass_matrix_C,
  D = missclass_matrix_D
)
```



## R Markdown

```{r}
#Simulated stable age distribution



# Ensure folder exists
dir.create("figures", showWarnings = FALSE)


# Create plot
p1 <- ggplot(dist_eigenvector, aes(x = age_labels, y = stable_dist)) +  # change 'value' if needed
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    x = "Age class",
    y = "Probability",
    title = "Stable age distribution"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)  # rotate 45 degrees
  )

# Save automatically
ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/age_distribution_p1.pdf",
  plot = p1,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)
p1
```
```{r}
# create msisclass matrix
missclass_matrix <-  truncated_misclassification(func_sigma = function(x) 4, func_mu = function(x) x+4/(x+0.5))

# convolute distribution analytically 
conv_data <- as.vector(t(missclass_matrix)%*%dist_eigenvector$stable_dist)

dist1 <- data.frame(
  age_labels = dist_eigenvector$age_labels,
  stable_dist = dist_eigenvector$stable_dist
) %>%
  mutate(source = "Stable age distribution")

dist2 <- data.frame(
  age_labels = dist_eigenvector$age_labels,  # same x-axis!
  stable_dist = conv_data
) %>%
  mutate(source = "Convoluted distribution")

combined <- bind_rows(dist2, dist1)

p2 <- ggplot(combined, aes(x = age_labels, y = stable_dist, fill = source)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.5) +  
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Distribution type",   # 👈 legend title
    values = c(
      "Convoluted distribution" = "Gray40",
      "Stable age distribution" = "Black"
    ) 
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Age class",
    y = "Probability",
    title = "Stable age vs convoluted age distribution"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
    theme(
    legend.position = c(0.8, 0.8),  # x=0.8 (right), y=0.8 (top)
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", size = 0.3),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.6, "lines")
  )


# Save automatically
ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/age_distribution_comp_p2.pdf",
  plot = p2,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)
p2

```
```{r}

# Standard deviations from 0.2 to 20
sigma_values <- seq(0.2, 10, length.out = 1000)

  

# Function to calculate condition number for a given sigma
calc_condition <- function(sigma) {
  mat <- truncated_misclassification(
    func_sigma = function(x) sigma,
    func_mu    = function(x) x
  )
  # Compute condition number
  kappa(mat)
}

# Apply function over all sigma values
cond_numbers <- map_dbl(sigma_values, calc_condition)

plot_data <- data.frame(
  sigma = sigma_values,
  variance = sigma_values^2,
  condition_number = cond_numbers
)

thresholds <- round(log(c(1, 5, 100)),3)

p3 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = sigma, y = condition_number)) +
  geom_point(color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = thresholds, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  annotate("text", x = c(0.5,2,3.5), y = thresholds, label = paste0("κ=", c(1,5,100)),
           hjust = 0, vjust = -0.3, size = 3.5, color = "gray40") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "Standard Deviation (σ)",
    y = "Logged Condition Number (κ)",
    title = "Logged Condition Number vs standard deviation of error"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/kondition_number_vs_sd_p3.pdf",
  plot = p3,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)
p3
```
## True distribution simulation
```{r}
# simulate 

sim_results_true_dist <- lapply( list(stable_dist), function(dist) {
  lapply(samples, function(n) {
    simulate_counts(dist, size_per_sample = n, n_samples = 100)
  })
})

#  summary statistic of TV from sim_results
tv_result_summary_true <-  compute_tv_all(sim_results_true_dist, stable_dist)

# convert to data frame
df_tv_true <- tv_to_df_summary(tv_result_summary_true)
df_tv_true$model <- "True distribution"
df_tv_true

# Export to ..\tables
table_tex <- kable(df_tv_true, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE)

writeLines(table_tex, "../tables/df_tv_true_t1.tex")
```


### Convolution data simulation
```{r}
# Show convolution 
set.seed(19911122)

# Convoluted Distribution
conv_dist <- lapply(missclass_all_t, function(M) {
  as.vector(M %*% stable_dist)
})

# Set samples
samples <- c(10, 25, 50, 100, 150, 200)

# To each type of conv matrix, sample for each type of sample size 
# Dist A,B,C,D = 1,2,3,4 & sampel size 10, 25, 50, 100 = 1, 2, 3, 4,5,6 Each one is a matrix where column is replica and row "bin" 
sim_results <- lapply(conv_dist, function(dist) {
  lapply(samples, function(n) {
    simulate_counts(dist, size_per_sample = n, n_samples = 100)
  })
})

#  summary statistic of TV from sim_results
tv_result_summary <-  compute_tv_all(sim_results, stable_dist)

# convert to data frame
df_tv_c <- tv_to_df_summary(tv_result_summary)

df_tv_c

# Export to ..\tables
table_tex <- kable(df_tv_c, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE)

writeLines(table_tex, "../tables/df_tv_convolution_t2.tex")

```

### Convolution graph
```{r}

df_all_c_t <- rbind(df_tv_c, df_tv_true)

p4 <- ggplot(df_all, aes(x = sample_size, y = mean, group = model, color = model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Convergence of TV Distance vs sample size",
    x = "Sample size",
    y = "Mean TV distance",
    color = "Error Model"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "A" = "red",
    "B" = "blue",
    "C" = "darkgreen",
    "D" = "orange",
    "True distribution" = "black"
  )) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 0.7)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)



ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/Conv_TV_vs_sample_p4.pdf",
  plot = p4,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)



p4
```
### Deconvolution uniform-init simulation 
```{r}
# Deconvolution steps
set.seed(19911122)

deconvolved_samples <- Map(function(dist_list, K) {
  lapply(dist_list, function(mat) {
    apply(mat, 2, function(col) {
      em_deconvolve(col, K = K)$p
    })
  })
}, sim_results, missclass_all)

#  summary statistic of TV from sim_results
tv_result_summary_deconv <-  compute_tv_all(deconvolved_samples, stable_dist)

# convert to data frame
df_tv_deconv_u <- tv_to_df_summary(tv_result_summary_deconv)

df_tv_deconv_u

# Export to ..\tables
table_tex <- kable(df_tv_deconv_u, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE)

writeLines(table_tex, "../tables/df_tv_deconvolution_u_t3.tex")
# 
# A	10	0.5635416	0.5442254	0.7641923
# A	25	0.4792911	0.4671815	0.7003950
# A	50	0.3984489	0.3892980	0.5646527
# A	100	0.3354296	0.3257366	0.4896924
# A	150	0.2741892	0.2598381	0.3893600
# A	200	0.2690260	0.2600026	0.3836192
# B	10	0.5363998	0.5455556	0.7712813
# B	25	0.4087443	0.4013897	0.6919004
# B	50	0.3180364	0.3045640	0.5465116
# B	100	0.2502946	0.2323665	0.4132018
```

### Deconvolution uniform graph
```{r}

df_all_du_t <- rbind(df_tv_deconv_u, df_tv_true)

p5 <- ggplot(df_all_du_t, aes(x = sample_size, y = mean, group = model, color = model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Sample size",
    y = "Mean TV distance",
    color = "Error Model"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "A" = "red",
    "B" = "blue",
    "C" = "darkgreen",
    "D" = "orange",
    "True distribution" = "black"
  )) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 0.7)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

p5

ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/deconv_TV_vs_sample_u_p5.pdf",
  plot = p5,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)

```
## Deconvolution well-informed init simulation

```{r}
# Deconvolution steps
set.seed(19911122)

deconvolved_samples_i <- Map(function(dist_list, K) {
  lapply(dist_list, function(mat) {
    apply(mat, 2, function(col) {
      em_deconvolve(col, K = K, init = TRUE)$p
    })
  })
}, sim_results, missclass_all)

#  summary statistic of TV from sim_results
tv_result_summary_deconv_i <-  compute_tv_all(deconvolved_samples_i, stable_dist)

# convert to data frame
df_tv_deconv_i <- tv_to_df_summary(tv_result_summary_deconv_i)

df_tv_deconv_i

# Export to ..\tables
table_tex <- kable(df_tv_deconv_i, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE)

writeLines(table_tex, "../tables/df_tv_deconvolution_i_t3.tex")
```
### Deconvolution informed - Graph
```{r}
df_all_di_i <- rbind(df_tv_deconv_i, df_tv_true)

p6 <- ggplot(df_all_di_i, aes(x = sample_size, y = mean, group = model, color = model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Sample size",
    y = "Mean TV distance",
    color = "Error Model"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "A" = "red",
    "B" = "blue",
    "C" = "darkgreen",
    "D" = "orange",
    "True distribution" = "black"
  )) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 0.7)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)


p6

ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/deconv_TV_vs_sample_i_p6.pdf",
  plot = p6,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)
  
```

```{r}
p7 <- (p5 | p6) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect")  + 
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Convergence of TV Distance vs sample size",
    tag_levels = "a"
  ) &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p7

ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/deconv_TV_vs_sample_p7.pdf",
  plot = p7,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)
```
```{r}
df_all <- rbind(
  df_tv_c %>% mutate(method = "Convolved"),
  df_tv_deconv_i %>% mutate(method = "Deconvolved informed"),
  df_tv_deconv_u %>% mutate(method = "Deconvolved uninformed")
)

ggplot(df_all, aes(x = factor(sample_size), y = mean, fill = method)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~model) +
  labs(
    x = "Sample size",
    y = "Mean TV distance",
    fill = "method"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Convolved" = "steelblue",
    "True / Convolved" = "black"
  )) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```
